title: Latent caution-state detector from field-wide lap anomalies
summary: Safety Car/VSC periods materially change pit economics by reducing effective pit loss.
rationale: Safety Car/VSC periods materially change pit economics by reducing effective pit loss. Public F1 strategy references emphasize opening Safety Car windows and discounted pit loss under SC/VSC, but the tabular data has no explicit caution flag.
implementation_hint: Infer a per-race-lap latent caution score from field-wide statistics already available in-data: mean/variance spikes in LapTime, compression in lap deltas, simultaneous position disruptions, and sudden multi-car pit bursts. Feed this score and its short lags into the main model or use it to gate a caution-specialist submodel.
expected_effect: Should capture a high-leverage regime missing from current features and reduce false negatives on opportunistic stops during neutralized conditions.
risk: Anomaly features may also fire on weather or incident-heavy laps, so the latent state can be noisy without robust smoothing.
